sites.name: Barnaul Cartridge Plant
sites.produces: Pistol cartridges (9x19 mm, 9x18 mm Makarov, .45 ACP), rifle cartridges (5.45x39, 7.62x39, 7.62x54R), 12-gauge shotgun shells; Bear, Centaur and Barnaul brands
sites.notes: Founded during WWII evacuation 1941; address: 28 Kulagina St., Barnaul 656002; coordinates approximate (city-level); Poly Technologies identified supplying gunpowder to plant June 2023; no reported Ukrainian strikes

sites.name: Klimovsk Specialized Ammunition Plant
sites.produces: Small-calibre cartridges: 7.62x39 mm, 9 mm pistol, underwater-weapons cartridges, hunting and sport ammunition
sites.notes: Founded 1936 as plant #711; Rostec rescued from bankruptcy mid-2024 and began modernisation; coordinates from latitude.to satellite map

sites.name: Murom Instrument-Making Plant
sites.produces: Ammunition primers (Berdan, Boxer, shotshell types); initiation and ignition devices for all calibres used by Russian Armed Forces, Navy, Air Force, MVD, and FSB

strikes.summary: SBU drones struck overnight; five explosions, two buildings damaged, fire broke out; ~70 workers evacuated.

11 drones in operation; 3 shot down, remainder struck targets; three buildings in industrial zone damaged; windows shattered in two workshops; ~70 workers evacuated; regional governor confirmed damage.

sites.notes: Russia's largest ammunition-primer producer; address: 30 Let Pobedy St. 1a, Murom 602205; ~700 km from Ukrainian border; sanctioned by Ukraine and EU since 2023; coordinates approximate (city of Murom)

sites.name: Novosibirsk Cartridge Plant
sites.produces: Small-arms cartridges (5.45x39, 7.62x39, 7.62x54R, 12.7x108 mm) for Russian Armed Forces and Rosgvardia; Sibfire brand sport and hunting ammunition
sites.notes: Founded 1939; address: 30A Stansionnaya St., Novosibirsk 630108; coordinates approximate (city-level); sanctioned by US (Dec 2023), EU, UK, Canada, Switzerland, Ukraine, Japan

sites.name: Tula Cartridge Plant
sites.produces: Small-arms cartridges (7.62 mm, 9 mm, 5.45 mm, 12-gauge) for Russian military and law enforcement; TulAmmo civilian brand; repurposed production lines making artillery rounds (~7,000/month)
sites.notes: Founded 1880; address: Marata St. 139, Tula 300004; coordinates approximate (city-level); sanctioned post-2022; artillery production lines added under wartime pressure with safety incidents reported

sites.name: Arzamas Machine-Building Plant
sites.produces: BTR-80 and BTR-82 armored personnel carriers; Tigr infantry mobility vehicles
sites.notes: Primary serial producer of BTR-80/82 family; estimated 500+ APCs per year; city-level coordinates (Arzamas city center approximate)

sites.name: Chelyabinsk Tractor Plant (ChTZ-Uraltrak)
sites.produces: V-92S2 diesel tank engines for T-72B3M and T-90M tanks (up to 1,000 hp)
sites.notes: Main supplier of tank engines for Uralvagonzavod; launched new ion-nitriding production section in February 2024 to triple engine output. 2023-11-26: explosion at transformer station, officially assessed as equipment failure, not confirmed attack.

sites.name: Kurganmashzavod
sites.produces: BMP-3 and BMP-2 infantry fighting vehicles, BMD-4 airborne IFVs
sites.notes: Sole serial producer of BMP-3 IFV; increased production 20% in 2024; running 12-hour shifts

sites.name: Motovilikha Plants
sites.produces: Tornado-G / Tornado-S multiple-launch rocket systems; Msta-B and Nona howitzers; self-propelled artillery
sites.notes: Expanded production facilities in 2024-2025; Putin visited September 2025; facility reorganized under new management in early 2025

sites.name: Omsktransmash
sites.produces: T-80BVM tank modernization; TOS-1A heavy flamethrower systems
sites.notes: Primary T-80 refurbishment and upgrade facility; city-level coordinates (address: Krasny pereulok 2, Omsk)

sites.name: Uralvagonzavod
sites.produces: T-90M and T-72B3M main battle tanks
sites.notes: Largest tank manufacturer in the world; operating 24-hour production cycle since 2022; delivered new batch of T-90M, T-80BVM, T-72B3M on Victory Day 2026

sites.name: Beriev Aircraft Company (TANTK)
sites.name_local: Таганрогский авиационный научно-технический комплекс им. Г. М. Бериева
sites.produces: A-50U AWACS repair/modernization; Tu-95/Tu-142 strategic bomber overhaul; Be-200 amphibious aircraft

strikes.summary: Large Ukrainian drone swarm struck Beriev plant workshops; A-50U under repair was damaged. HUR confirmed successful strike; Ukrainian spokesman called it 'incredibly successful.' Russia claimed drones were repelled, contradicted by evidence.

Ukrainian drone strike destroyed the Beriev A-60 airborne laser aircraft (one of only two prototypes ever built) and an Il-76 on the facility's airfield, plus caused structural damage to the final assembly shop. Confirmed by satellite imagery.

Ukrainian Unmanned Systems Forces destroyed two Tu-142 aircraft undergoing repair at the Taganrog facility (including a rare Tu-142MR submarine communications relay aircraft) and an Iskander missile launcher.

sites.notes: Most-struck Russian aviation repair facility; lost A-50U, A-60 laser aircraft prototype, and two Tu-142s since 2024

sites.name: Irkutsk Aviation Plant
sites.produces: Su-30SM2 multirole fighters; Yak-130 jet trainers
sites.notes: Delivered upgraded Su-30SM2 fighters to Russian Air Force in August 2024; also supplies Su-30SM to export clients

sites.name: Kazan Aircraft Production Association (KAPO)
sites.name_local: Казанское авиационное производственное объединение имени С. П. Горбунова
sites.produces: Tu-160M strategic bombers (refurbishment and new-build from Soviet airframes); Tu-214 airliners

strikes.summary: Ukrainian long-range kamikaze drones struck the Kazan aviation plant. No damage details publicly confirmed by either side at time of reporting.

Ukrainian kamikaze drones struck the factory airfield at approximately 05:00; fire broke out near KAPO-Composite hangar (fuel tank hit at coordinates 55.867 deg N 49.114 deg E). Airport suspended operations. Russian MoD claimed all drones were intercepted, contradicted by visible evidence.

sites.notes: Russia cannot build entirely new Tu-160/Tu-22M3 from scratch; uses Soviet-era unfinished airframes; 8,500 employees

sites.name: Kazan Helicopters
sites.produces: Mi-8/Mi-17 transport helicopters; Mi-38 medium helicopter; Ansat light helicopter
sites.notes: World's largest Mi-8/17 producer; delivered over 12,000 helicopters; planned production of 37+ Mi-8MTV-1 in 2026; coordinates approximate (western Kazan, Tetsovskaya St 14)

sites.name: UEC-Klimov
sites.produces: TV3-117 / VK-2500 turboshaft engines for Mi-8/Mi-24/Mi-28/Ka-52 helicopters; RD-33 turbofan for MiG-29
sites.notes: Produces ~100 VK-2500 engines/year for combat helicopters; developing VK-650V to replace Western engines in Ansat helicopter

sites.name: Komsomolsk-on-Amur Aircraft Plant (KnAAZ)
sites.name_local: Комсомольский-на-Амуре авиационный завод имени Ю. А. Гагарина
sites.produces: Su-57 fifth-generation stealth fighters; Su-35S multirole fighters
sites.notes: Russia's sole serial Su-57 producer; by late 2025 only ~20-25 Su-57s delivered vs. 76-aircraft contract; new facilities opened for Su-57 fuel systems in 2024. 2026-04-11: massive fire destroyed Shop 46 (Su-57 composite components); cause not officially confirmed, no attack claim found in cited sources.

sites.name: Novosibirsk Aircraft Production Association (NAZ Chkalov)
sites.name_local: Новосибирский авиационный завод имени В. П. Чкалова
sites.produces: Su-34 fighter-bomber; repairs and modernization of Su-24M
sites.notes: Sole serial producer of Su-34; delivered four batches of Su-34M in 2024; operating on three shifts with doubled pre-war production rate

sites.name: Rostvertol
sites.produces: Mi-28N/NM attack helicopters; Mi-35M combat helicopters; Mi-26T heavy transport helicopters
sites.notes: World leader in heavy-lift helicopter manufacture; debris from December 2024 Rostov drone raid reported near plant perimeter - no confirmed direct strike on facility

sites.name: UEC Saturn (NPO Saturn)
sites.produces: AL-31 series / AL-41 turbofans for Su-30/Su-35/Su-57; ship propulsion gas turbines
sites.notes: ~23,000 employees; also produces SaM146 / PowerJet engines for Superjet 100; coordinates approximate (city-level, Rybinsk)

sites.name: Ulan-Ude Aviation Plant (UUAP)
sites.produces: Mi-8T / Mi-171 / Mi-171Sh transport and assault helicopters
sites.notes: Has produced over 4,000 Mi-8 family helicopters; major export supplier; delivered 850th Mi-171 series helicopter during war period

sites.name: Ufa Engine Industrial Association (UMPO)
sites.produces: AL-31F/AL-41F1S turbofan engines for Su-27/Su-30/Su-34/Su-35/Su-57 family
sites.notes: Largest aero-engine complex in Russia; also produces helicopter transmission components; coordinates approximate (city-level, Ferina St 2, Ufa)

sites.name: Yelabuga Drone Factory (Alabuga SEZ)
sites.produces: Shahed-136/Geran-2 loitering munitions and Albatross reconnaissance drones

strikes.summary: Ukrainian forces attacked using a modified civilian aircraft drone; local officials disputed extent of damage but a fire was reported at the site.
Ukrainian A-22 towing unpowered glider struck final assembly shops, causing large-scale explosions and fires; one worker killed, 13 injured. Tatarstan head confirmed the attack.

sites.notes: Producing ~170 Geran-2 drones/day as of spring 2025; ~26,000 total produced by mid-2025. Rapid expansion underway with hundreds of new residential and industrial buildings.

sites.name: Izhevsk Electromechanical Plant (IEMZ Kupol)
sites.produces: Garpiya-A1 (Shahed-type) kamikaze drones; Tor-M1/M2 and Osa-AKM SAM systems

strikes.summary: Ukrainian drones struck the plant, damaging equipment in Tor missile and radar production workshops; one worker hospitalized. Evacuation of personnel reported.

Ukrainian Liutyi long-range drones struck four drone production workshops, halting operations. Three workers killed, 45 injured (35 hospitalized). Ukraine's General Staff confirmed the strike 1,300 km from front lines.

sites.notes: Has a 2025 contract for 6,000+ Garpiya drones (up from 2,000 in 2024); also Russia's second Shahed-type production site alongside Alabuga.

sites.name: Kronshtadt Group – Dubna Factory
sites.produces: Orion/Inokhodets MALE strike drones; Sirius UAV; Banderol cruise missile

strikes.summary: Ukrainian Liutyi drones struck the factory in a 155-drone night campaign; eight hits recorded on the workshop roof. Satellite imagery confirmed one-third or more of the final assembly hall destroyed. Third strike on the site overall.

sites.notes: Facility opened 2022 ($53 m investment); AO Kronshtadt near bankruptcy by August 2025 after strikes and mounting debts. Coordinates approximate (city-level Dubna).

sites.name: ZALA Aero Group – Izhevsk Production Facility
sites.produces: Lancet-1 and Lancet-3 loitering munitions; ZALA reconnaissance UAVs
sites.notes: Primary production at 40 Let Pobedy St 118, Izhevsk; supplemental production in converted shopping centres (Italmas, Novy Dom area). Novy Dom fire on 2024-02-17 (cause unconfirmed by Ukraine). Production tripled since 2022; ~7,000 Lancets used against Ukraine by late 2024.

sites.name: Karachev Plant Elektrodetal
sites.produces: Electrical connectors (25,000+ types) for aerospace, EW, radar, avionics; supplies 1,500+ Russian defence enterprises

strikes.summary: Four Ukrainian R-360 Neptune cruise missiles struck from 240 km; large-scale fire broke out, potentially causing complete indefinite shutdown of operations.

sites.notes: Part of KRET/Rostec; located ~113 km from Ukraine border. First known major direct strike on this facility was September 2025.

sites.name: Krasnogorsky Zavod (KMZ / Zenit)
sites.produces: Military optical sights, fire-control systems for tanks and armoured vehicles, laser designators, aircraft surveillance systems (OPS-28M for Mi-28NM)
sites.notes: 30 km from Moscow Red Square; founded 1941 as defence optics factory. Coordinates approximate (city-level).

sites.name: Kremniy El Microelectronics Plant
sites.produces: Military-grade microchips, power modules, semiconductor devices for Iskander, Pantsir, S-500, Kalibr systems

strikes.summary: Ukrainian drones struck the plant overnight; fire broke out in attic of a non-residential building, between 10-15 explosions recorded near the facility. Described as the fourth UAV attack on the enterprise.
Six drones struck production areas and storage facilities overnight; plant halted operations, power supply and assembly lines disrupted. No casualties reported.
Fifth recorded strike on the plant; 2-3 hits confirmed in factory workshop amid a 115-drone attack on Bryansk region. One person killed, one injured.

Ukraine struck the plant with British-supplied Storm Shadow missiles; at least four large blasts recorded, 7 people killed, 29 hospitalized. Ukraine described it as part of systematic effort to reduce Russia's military-economic potential.

sites.notes: Supplies 90-94% of output to Russian Ministry of Defence; 800+ defense clients. Located ~110 km from Ukraine border.

sites.name: Mikron Semiconductor Plant (Elma Technopark)
sites.produces: Military-grade integrated circuits, RFID chips, power semiconductors; chips used in missile and payment systems

strikes.summary: Ukrainian strike drones hit the Elma Technopark housing Mikron; at least one building damaged by explosion and fire. Moscow mayor reported no serious casualties.
Repeat drone strike on Elma Technopark overnight 16-17 May 2026; fire at facility, powerful explosion reported by local residents. Part of a ~600-drone assault on Moscow region confirmed by Zelensky.

sites.notes: Russia's largest domestic microelectronics manufacturer; 37 km northwest of Moscow centre. Technopark covers 60,000 sq m with 150+ resident firms.

sites.name: Ryazan State Instrument Plant (GRPZ)
sites.produces: Airborne weapons-control radars for Su-27, Su-30, Su-35, MiG-29, Su-57; helicopter radars for Mi-28N, Mi-35
sites.notes: Largest aircraft instrument manufacturer in Russia; no confirmed direct strikes as of June 2026. Coordinates approximate (city-level, Seminarskaya St 32).

sites.name: Ural Optical-Mechanical Plant (UOMZ)
sites.produces: Military aircraft optical systems: bomb aiming, laser rangefinder-designators, targeting pods for Su-27, MiG-29, Tu-160, Mi-28NM
sites.notes: One of Russia's leading military optics enterprises; Yekaterinburg previously considered beyond drone range but now acknowledged as threatened as of 2026.

sites.name: VNIIR-Progress Plant
sites.produces: Kometa CRPA anti-jamming GNSS antennas for Shahed drones, Iskander-M, Kalibr missiles, UMPK guided bomb kits

strikes.summary: Two AN-196 Liutyi drones struck the facility after flying ~975 km, sparking a fire and causing plant to suspend operations temporarily.
Repeat drone strike on VNIIR-Progress production workshop after June 2025 attack.
Ukrainian FP-5 Flamingo cruise missile struck the facility; powerful explosion, thick smoke visible across Cheboksary, significant damage to main workshop.
Second Flamingo strike within one week; satellite imagery showed main workshop practically completely destroyed, missile penetrated front of the building.

sites.notes: Located ~1,000 km from Ukraine border; Kometa antennas allow Russian guided weapons to resist Ukrainian electronic warfare jamming.

sites.name: Aleksin Chemical Plant
sites.produces: Nitrocellulose (pyroxyline gunpowder) and chemical precursors for gunpowder and weapons charges; supplies Russian cartridge and ammunition plants

strikes.summary: SSU and Special Operations Forces launched at least 13 drones; gunpowder production shop caught fire with orange acid smoke; second explosion at 13:10; 110 kV power line damaged; operations halted and staff evacuated.
DIU drones struck an underground pyroxyline gunpowder depot at the plant; large fire and powerful explosions reported by local residents.

sites.notes: Sanctioned by US (2023) and EU (Feb 2024); address: 21 Pobedy Square, Aleksin 301361; ~320 km from Ukrainian border; coordinates from Yandex/latitude.to for city of Aleksin

sites.name: Biysk Oleum Plant (BOZ)
sites.produces: TNT and HMX (octogen) military explosives; new RDX (hexogen) production line under construction rated 6,000 tonnes/year
sites.notes: Remote Siberian location (~3,000 km from Moscow) deliberately chosen to evade Ukrainian drone range; construction value 15.5 billion rubles; Reuters confirmed via procurement documents; coordinates from wikimapia warehouse reference (52 deg 29'N 85 deg 0'E)

sites.name: Kazan State Gunpowder Plant
sites.produces: Pyroxylin gunpowder and propellant charges for small arms (5.45-14.5 mm), artillery (76-152 mm), tank ammunition (125 mm T-72/T-80/T-90), naval and aviation weapons; ~20% of Russia's army powder charges
sites.notes: Oldest Russian gunpowder plant (founded 1786); EU and US sanctioned 2023; fire in propellant charge workshop reportedly cut production 20-30%; address: Pervogo Maya St. 14, Kazan 420032. 2026-04-14: building destroyed in explosion officially classified as technogenic (pressure-relief system failure); no attack claimed.

sites.name: Perm Powder Plant
sites.produces: Gunpowder and solid-fuel propellant charges for Grad, Smerch, Tornado MLRS; Pantsir-S and other air-defence missiles; Kh-59 series air-launched cruise missiles; naval munitions; components for Topol-M and Bulava strategic missiles
sites.notes: One of Russia's largest defence-sector powder plants; address: Galperina St. 11, Perm 614101; produces components for 100+ weapon systems; ~1,700 km from Ukrainian border; under US, EU, UK sanctions; coordinates from Yandex Maps for address

sites.name: Promsintez JSC
sites.produces: Military and industrial explosives: ammonal, ammonite, granulite, TNT; supplied to equip artillery shells, aerial bombs, and missiles; >30,000 tonnes/year capacity

strikes.summary: SBU loitering munitions flew ~1,500 km to strike the plant; at least 20 explosions and several fires recorded; ~1 tonne of nitric acid spilled; management suspended all production.
Ukrainian FP-5 Flamingo cruise missile struck the explosives synthesis production area; explosion confirmed on video by local witnesses; follows the April 2025 production halt.

sites.notes: Leading CIS explosives manufacturer; located ~850 km from Ukrainian border; coordinates from OSINT-confirmed strike location (52.942, 49.694)

sites.name: Ya. M. Sverdlov Plant
sites.produces: RDX (hexogen) and HMX (octogen) military-grade explosives; aerial bombs; artillery shell warheads; anti-tank and air-defence missile warheads

strikes.summary: Dozens of Ukrainian-made strike drones operated by SSU, SOF, and DIU struck overnight; thick smoke column over plant; four fire-station employees lightly wounded.
Three drones damaged a pipeline trestle overpass and two workshops; hexogen/octogen production workshop confirmed hit by Ukrainian intelligence sources.
Wave of ~20 Ukrainian drones struck the plant; 25-30 explosions reported by residents, large fire visible across the city; Ukraine General Staff confirmed the strike.

sites.notes: Russia's only producer of HMX and RDX; 1,100-hectare site with ~5,000 employees; ~900 km from Ukrainian border; coordinates from OSINT (Tendar/X: 56°16'6.29"N 43°22'43.93"E)

sites.name: Tambov Gunpowder Plant
sites.produces: Smokeless pyroxylin powder for small arms, artillery shells, mortar rounds, grenade launchers, and tank rounds; nitrocellulose; over 200 product types

strikes.summary: Ukrainian military intelligence (HUR) drone attack on the facility; fire and disruption reported.
HUR kamikaze drones struck the plant in Kotovsk; explosion confirmed on video; Russian officials denied damage but production disruption reported.
Overnight drone strike triggered up to 15 explosions; fire broke out; operations paused per Ukraine's Center for Countering Disinformation.
Drone attack on the Kotovsk plant sparked a confirmed fire; OSINT analysts verified footage as authentic.

sites.notes: One of Russia's largest smokeless-powder producers; ~360 km from Ukrainian border; production increased ~one-third since Feb 2022; coordinates approximate (city of Kotovsk)

sites.name: Arzamas Instrument-Building Plant
sites.name_local: АО «Арзамасский приборостроительный завод им. П.И. Пландина»
sites.produces: Gyroscopic instruments, control systems, onboard computers and steering gears for Kh-101 and Kh-32 cruise missiles

strikes.summary: Four SBU Special Operations Center 'A' long-range drones struck production facilities at 3:55 a.m. Roof damaged, windows shattered, equipment destroyed. One worker killed, two injured. Russian governor Nikitin confirmed the attack on two industrial zones.

sites.notes: Located at 50 Let VLKSM St 8a, Arzamas. 759 km from Ukraine's border. ~90% of output under state defense contracts; over 6,000 employees. On EU, US, Ukraine, and New Zealand sanctions lists.

sites.name: VMP Avitek – Kirov Plant
sites.produces: 9M334 Tor SAM missile modules; S-400 component missiles; aircraft armaments and ejection seats
sites.notes: Located at Oktyabrsky Prospekt 1A, Kirov. Coordinates approximate (city-level). One of three key Almaz-Antey plants modernized for S-400 surge production. Added to EU sanctions March 2024.

sites.name: Dolgoprudnenskoe Scientific Production Plant (DNPP)
sites.produces: Buk-M1-2 surface-to-air missiles (9M317, 9M38M1); Shtil-1 naval SAMs
sites.notes: Located at Sobina Square 1, Dolgoprudny, Moscow Oblast. Founded 1931 (originally dirigible production); producing SAMs since 1960. Coordinates approximate (city-level Dolgoprudny).

sites.name: KBP Instrument Design Bureau – Shcheglovsky Val Plant
sites.produces: Pantsir-S1 short-range SAM/gun systems; Kornet ATGM; Krasnopol guided shells; Tunguska AAA

strikes.summary: Ukrainian Defense Intelligence drones struck the Shcheglovsky Val plant, causing explosions and fire in the Proletarian district. Ukraine confirmed the attack on Pantsir production facilities.
Ukrainian drones struck two defense enterprises simultaneously — KBP and NPO Splav — causing fires and damage confirmed by Cyberboroshno satellite analysis.
Ukrainian drones struck KBP twice within 72 hours (June 18 and 20), destroying electrical substation, storage warehouses, administrative building, and a loading hangar. Plant halted production.

sites.notes: Coordinates from Cyberboroshno OSINT geolocation (54.226747, 37.697645). Founded 1927; ~8,600 employees. Also known as Shipunov KBP.

sites.name: MKB Raduga (Bereznyak Machine-Building Design Bureau)
sites.produces: Kh-101/Kh-55 strategic cruise missiles; Kh-69, Kh-59MK, Kh-32 air-to-surface missiles

strikes.summary: SBU long-range drones bypassed Moscow air defences and struck assembly and equipment workshops, setting them on fire. Part of Ukraine's largest-ever drone campaign (296 UAVs total that night). SBU confirmed the strike.

sites.notes: Plant located in Dubna, 130 km north of Moscow, at Zhukovsky St 2a. Produces cruise missiles Russia uses in daily strikes on Ukraine.

sites.name: Nizhny Novgorod 70th Anniversary of Victory Plant
sites.produces: S-400 Triumf air defense missile systems; S-500 Prometey; Vityaz S-350; Nebo-M and Nioby radars
sites.notes: Located at Sormovskoe Shosse 21, Nizhny Novgorod. Opened 2016 by Putin; part of major Almaz-Antey S-400 production surge (output reportedly 4x higher in 2025 vs pre-war). Coordinates reference Nizhny Novgorod Machine-building Plant in Sormovo district (same campus area).

sites.name: NPO Novator (OKB Novator)
sites.produces: 3M54 Kalibr cruise missile family (ship/sub/air-launched); 9M729 ground-launched cruise missile; S-300V missiles
sites.notes: Co-located with Kalinin Machine-Building Plant (MZiK) at Prospekt Kosmonavtov 18, Yekaterinburg. ~30 Kalibr missiles/month production rate by late 2024. Never publicly struck; deep in Urals.

sites.name: NPO Splav – Tula MLRS Plant
sites.produces: BM-21 Grad, BM-30 Smerch, Tornado-G/S multiple launch rocket systems and rockets; TOS-1A heavy flamethrower rockets

strikes.summary: Ukrainian drones struck a production building of NPO Splav, confirmed by Cyberboroshno OSINT with photographic evidence showing smoke and fires about 330 km from Ukraine.
Second strike that month; a functioning workshop and an electrical substation caught fire. Damage confirmed by local reports.

sites.notes: Russia's only MLRS designer-producer. Address: Shcheglovsky Val area, Tula. Coordinates from OSINT reporting (54.222495, 37.692874).

sites.name: Obukhov State Plant (GOZ Obukhovsky Plant)
sites.produces: S-400 Triumf and S-350 Poliment-Redut SAM missile launchers and components; S-300V4 systems; S-500 components
sites.notes: Located at Obukhovskoy Oborony pr. 120, St Petersburg. Doubled S-400/S-350 missile output in 2025. Ukrainian ATESH partisans conducted reconnaissance in May 2025 but no strike reported.

sites.name: PO Strela – Orenburg Production Association
sites.produces: P-800 Oniks anti-ship cruise missile components; Su/MiG aircraft systems; Kh-35 missile components
sites.notes: Located at Shevchenko St 26, Orenburg (460005). ATESH partisans scouted plant coordinates in November 2025. Coordinates approximate (city-level Orenburg). 2026-05-12: Ukrainian drone struck residential building ~4 km from the plant; no direct hit on the plant confirmed.

sites.name: Smolensk Aviation Plant
sites.produces: Kh-59 Ovod air-to-surface missiles; Kh-55 strategic cruise missiles; Kh-101 components

strikes.summary: Ukrainian Chaklun-V long-range kamikaze UAVs struck production infrastructure; one drone hit the production building while a second was shot down. GUR confirmed the attack disrupted Kh-59 production.
Explosions and smoke reported at the aviation plant; OSINT confirmed strike location near production facilities. Local residents documented the event.

sites.notes: Located at Frunze St 74, Smolensk, SE of Smolensk North Airport. On EU, US, and Ukrainian sanctions lists.

sites.name: Tactical Missiles Corporation HQ – Korolyov
sites.produces: Kh-31, Kh-35, Kh-38, Kh-29 air-to-surface missiles; R-77/R-73 air-to-air missiles; corporate HQ with R&D integration
sites.notes: Headquarters and key R&D/integration facility in Korolyov (formerly Kaliningrad). Coordinates approximate (city-level). Production 5x pre-war levels as of early 2024. Subsidiaries include MKB Raduga, Smolensk Aviation Plant, Vympel NPO.

sites.name: Votkinsk Machine-Building Plant
sites.produces: Iskander-M and Iskander-K ballistic/cruise missiles; Oreshnik IRBM; RS-24 Yars ICBM components

strikes.summary: Ukrainian FP-5 Flamingo cruise missiles struck the plant ~1,300 km from Ukraine's border; workshops No. 22 and 36 confirmed destroyed. Satellite imagery showed a 30x24 m breach in building No. 19. Local Udmurtia head confirmed damage and casualties.

sites.notes: Deepest-ever Ukrainian strike on a Russian production facility at time of attack. Plant also produces Kinzhal and Bulava components.

sites.name: KamAZ Truck Plant
sites.produces: Heavy military trucks and logistics vehicles for the Russian Army (Typhoon MRAP, standard tactical trucks)
sites.notes: Russia's largest heavy vehicle manufacturer; main supplier of trucks to the Russian military. Plant sprawls over 57 sq km. Coordinates approximate (city-level). 2024-04-28: fire reported near KamAZ; cause unconfirmed, KamAZ denied fire was on their premises.

sites.name: Admiralty Shipyards
sites.produces: Lada-class diesel-electric submarines; Kilo-class submarines; 70% of production volume is submarine construction
sites.notes: Delivered Yakutsk and Velikiye Luki (Lada-class) in 2025; two more Lada-class submarines laid down in 2026. No confirmed Ukrainian strikes as of June 2026; located deep inside Russia.

sites.name: Sevastopol Shipyard (Sevmorzavod)
sites.produces: Repair and modernisation of Black Sea Fleet warships and submarines; limited new construction (tankers, barges)

strikes.summary: Ukraine struck the shipyard with 10 Storm Shadow cruise missiles and 3 unmanned surface vessels; landing ship Minsk and Kilo-class submarine Rostov-on-Don destroyed in dry dock, 24 personnel injured, large fire.

sites.notes: Main repair base for Russia's Black Sea Fleet; some facilities reported halted by 2025. Strike destroyed two warships considered irreplaceable in Russia's current shipbuilding capacity.

sites.name: Sevmash Shipyard
sites.produces: Nuclear submarines (Borei-A SSBN, Yasen-M SSGN); Russia's only producer of nuclear-powered submarines
sites.notes: Largest shipbuilding enterprise in Russia; sole nuclear submarine producer. Currently building 8-10 submarines simultaneously, including Khabarovsk (Poseidon carrier) launched November 2025. Located in Severodvinsk on White Sea, ~1,600 km from front lines.

sites.name: Zaliv Shipyard
sites.produces: Large amphibious assault ships (Project 23900 Ivan Rogov-class); vessel repair and construction

strikes.summary: Ukraine struck Zaliv shipyard complex in Kerch with French SCALP cruise missiles; Project 22800 corvette Askold undergoing final testing was significantly damaged in its upper section.

sites.notes: Constructing Ivan Rogov-class $1.2B amphibious assault ship (commissioned target delayed to ~2028). Russia installed Soviet-era AK-630 AA gun and barge/net barriers to defend dry dock from Ukrainian USVs.
